responses.reply: Voter registration is the process of adding an eligible citizen to the electoral roll. In India, voters typically use NVSP or official ECI-linked services to apply, update details, and check enrollment status.
Verification confirms that the voter is listed correctly and can be identified at the polling station. Voters should check their electoral roll entry and carry an accepted identity document.
On voting day, voters visit their assigned polling station, verify identity, receive permission to vote, and cast a ballot using the voting system provided at the booth.
After polling ends, votes are counted under official supervision. Results are declared after counting, verification, and completion of required formalities.
A typical election timeline includes announcement, nominations, campaigning, voting, counting, and result declaration. Each stage has official rules and deadlines.

fallbackReply: I can help with voter registration, verification, voting day, election timelines, counting, results, and common scenarios. Try asking about one of those topics.
